name: Neighborhood Housing Services Of Southern Nevada
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor offering down payment assistance, homebuyer education, and foreclosure prevention for low- to moderate-income Southern Nevada residents since 1992.
description_long:
Neighborhood Housing Services of Southern Nevada (NHSSN) was founded in 1992 with a mission to enhance the well-being of low- to moderate-income individuals and communities in Southern Nevada. The organization has grown into a comprehensive housing support provider, distributing over $15 million in down payment assistance and helping 1,200 families avoid foreclosure. During the COVID-19 pandemic, NHSSN distributed $3.9 million in emergency aid to affected residents.

NHSSN offers a broad range of services across the housing lifecycle. For renters, they provide affordable housing starting at $1,046/month through properties like The Betty-Jeanne and 500 W Jefferson. For prospective homebuyers, they deliver down payment assistance programs and homebuyer education through both in-person sessions and online platforms via E-Home America. For current homeowners, they provide counseling on equity leverage, refinancing, reverse mortgages, and foreclosure prevention. All education and counseling services are personalized one-on-one sessions.

NHSSN distinguishes itself through its multi-service, housing-first approach and deep community roots spanning over 30 years. The organization supports residents across multiple languages (Spanish, French, Russian, Tagalog, and Chinese), indicating accessibility for diverse populations. Their documented impact—1,300 new homeowners assisted and $15 million in down payment aid—demonstrates sustained, measurable community outcomes. The combination of direct housing provision, financial counseling, and emergency assistance during crisis periods shows operational sophistication.

As a non-profit, NHSSN provides legitimate, non-predatory assistance aligned with HUD standards. However, the website lacks specific details on eligibility requirements, application timelines, counselor certifications (NFCC status), interest rates on any financing products, or detailed program terms. Prospective clients would need to contact the organization directly for comprehensive program details and personalized assessments.
pros: HUD-aligned non-profit with 30+ year track record since 1992 providing housing solutions
$15 million in documented down payment assistance awarded to 1,300+ homeowners
Multilingual support in Spanish, French, Russian, Tagalog, and Chinese for diverse populations
Comprehensive services spanning renters, homebuyers, and existing homeowners in one organization
Personalized one-on-one counseling sessions rather than group-only approach
Affordable rental housing starting at $1,046/month for low- to moderate-income residents
Emergency relief track record: $3.9 million distributed during COVID-19 pandemic
cons: Website lacks specific eligibility income thresholds or asset limits for programs
No published information on counselor certifications, credentials, or NFCC affiliation status
Limited detail on down payment assistance terms (repayment requirements, forgiveness conditions, maximum amounts)
Geographically limited to Southern Nevada service area only
Application and processing timeline information not disclosed on website
best_for: Low- to moderate-income first-time homebuyers in Southern Nevada seeking down payment help
Homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking refinancing guidance in the Las Vegas area
Renters searching for affordable housing ($1K-$1.5K range) in safe, managed communities
Non-English speakers needing housing counseling in Spanish, French, Russian, Tagalog, or Chinese
diagnosis: NHSSN is best for low- to moderate-income Southern Nevada residents seeking legitimate, non-predatory assistance with homeownership, foreclosure prevention, or affordable rental housing. The main caveat is that services are geographically restricted to Southern Nevada and prospective clients must contact the organization directly to confirm eligibility and obtain program-specific details not published online.
services: Down payment assistance program for homebuyers with low to moderate income
Homebuyer education courses (in-person and online through E-Home America)
Foreclosure prevention counseling and homeowner assistance
Affordable rental housing management and leasing
Multilingual counseling (Spanish, French, Russian, Tagalog, Chinese)
Community building and resident support programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1849 Civic Center Dr, North Las Vegas, NV 89030
